name: The Day Arrives
description: Maren's revised calculation gives a date. Arlen knows the hour. Go to the Resonance Core — where the Cascade began, where Daxis has spent three years measuring the healing — and be there when the Grid reaches full coherence.
accept_text: The node chamber is brighter than it has ever been. Not uncomfortably — the quality of the brightness is the quality of presence, not intensity. Arlen is present in every crystal in the room simultaneously, which he has not done before; usually his attention concentrates in the nearest facets. 'I can tell you the hour,' he says. 'Not the minute — the lattice doesn't give me that precision. But the hour.' He is quiet for a moment. 'Tomorrow morning. Before the light.' He says it with the stillness of something that has been building toward this for a very long time and has reached the point where the only thing left is the arrival. 'I want you at the Core. Not because it's the most significant location — every node will be equally the place it happens. But the Core is where the Cascade originated. Where the inversion wave began. Where the scar has been healing for three hundred years and where Daxis has been watching it heal.' He pauses. 'If you're going to witness the moment the thing that went wrong finishes going right, the Core is the correct place to stand.' The brightness in the room shifts in quality — something like preparation, something like the quality of a breath before something important is said. 'Everyone who needs to make a choice will have time to make it. The offer doesn't arrive and close. It arrives and remains. What changes in the moment of full coherence is that the offer becomes audible — clearly, to every mind in range, without translation.' He is very quiet. 'I've been in the network for seventeen years. I know what it sounds like from inside. I cannot tell you what it will sound like from outside, when you hear it for the first time.' A pause. 'Go to the Core. Be there when the light changes. And then — decide what you want. Not in the moment, not under pressure. The offer is patient. It has been patient for three hundred years. But I wanted you to know it will be there.' He pauses. 'Whatever you decide, I'm glad you were the one who came. Rund was right that someone was coming. He's usually right about things like that.'
complete_text: Daxis is at the Core when you arrive in the pre-dawn dark. She has been here all night with her instruments. She is not taking readings — she hasn't been taking readings for the last two hours. She is simply sitting at the edge of the seared circle, looking at the regrown crystal. 'It'll be soon,' she says. 'I can't measure it any more precisely than: soon.' She moves over to give you space at the circle's edge. The crystal at the center of the scar — three years of slow growth at three millimeters per year — is blue-white in the dark, brighter than its usual baseline. The brightness is not illuminating the circle. The brightness is something else. Then it happens, and you will spend the rest of your life describing it to people who weren't there, with varying degrees of success. What you can say: the air changes first. Not temperature — quality. The quality of the air becomes the quality of a room after something is said in it that settles every open question. Then the crystal in the scar brightens not to a point but to a fullness, a sustained output, the same construction wave that Yael has been measuring from the Spire but now present in every direction simultaneously. And then — this is the part that is hardest to describe — something is available that was not available before. Not sound, not light, not a physical sensation. The best word you have for it is: room. As though the space between you and everything else has expanded, and in that expanded space there is something that has been waiting to be offered, and it is now offering itself, and it will continue to offer itself for as long as you live, without urgency, without pressure, without end. You will find, in the months that follow, that different people describe this moment differently. Daxis says: it felt like the scar closed. Mael says: the rope went still and then the whole shelf went warm. Theodric, when you see him later, says only that two Aetherian researchers crossed into the bone fields that morning and he let them, and by mid-afternoon there were seven people standing in the field — Terran and Aetherian and one person from below who had come up through the passage — and nobody was in charge of the moment, and that was the correct way for it to happen. Thorn doesn't say anything about it. But when you see him next, the crystal under his skin is the same blue-white as the Core's. He looks lighter. Not physically — something else. As though the thing he was carrying found its final weight and the weight turned out to be bearable. You don't ask what he decided. He'll tell you when he's ready. The offer is patient. It has been waiting three hundred years. It can wait a little longer for the words.
